validator_assessment.weakest_link: Agents treat flat VIX + weak tape as confirmation of continued selling, but flat VIX after a weak close is historically correlated with short-covering at support — macro agent explicitly admits 'flat VIX leaves room for consolidation or reversal,' yet consensus remains bearish. Level_thesis also contradicts itself by identifying the primary level scenario as bullish (support hold → 696.53).
validator_assessment.top_invalidation_scenario: 691.66 support holds AND price bounces above 693.00 by 09:55 ET — directly flips consensus to bullish per level_thesis's own primary scenario.

primary.condition: if SPY breaks below 691.66 Active support with volume confirmation

secondary.condition: if SPY tests and holds 691.66 then reclaims 693.00 psychological level

invalidation.condition: 691.66 support holds AND price bounces above 693.00 by 09:55 ET (first 25 min of RTH)
invalidation.effect: Bearish consensus collapses — level_thesis's own primary scenario (support hold → 696.53) activates; BEAR ribbon would face compression from below the pivot; consider flipping to bullish puts-to-calls.

swarm_predictions.claim: SPY touches or breaks 691.66 Active support within the first 90 minutes of RTH (by 11:00 ET) — BEAR ribbon at 72c spread and current price 45c above this level make an early test near-certain
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY does not trade at or below 691.66 before 11:00 ET (price rallies directly from open toward 693+ without testing support)

swarm_predictions.claim: VIX remains below 19.5 throughout the RTH session — flat VIX at 17.86 with no scheduled macro events and overnight gap flat signals no new panic premium entering; VIX stays range-bound 16.5–19.5
swarm_predictions.invalidation: VIX prints above 19.5 by 10:05 ET, confirming capitulation and validating full bearish thesis with conviction

swarm_predictions.claim: BEAR ribbon (72c spread, fast 693.04 < pivot 693.23 < slow 693.76) does not flip to BULL in the first 60 minutes of RTH — a sustained 5m close above 693.23 pivot is required and BEAR momentum from two high-volume closing bars (121k + 204k) prevents this
swarm_predictions.invalidation: SPY closes a 5m bar above 693.23 (ribbon pivot) within the first 60 minutes of RTH (by 10:30 ET), signaling ribbon compression toward a potential bull flip

synthesis_narrative: The central tension on 2026-02-11 is whether the BEAR ribbon + high-conviction closing volume (121k/204k on the final two 5m bars) represents a true selling continuation or merely a squeeze zone with an immediate support test at 691.66 (45 bps below open). All 4 specialists voted bearish with aligned reasoning — weak tape (SPY -0.41%, XLK -0.88%), BEAR ribbon (72c spread), and structural positioning below the pivot — but the validator correctly flags that flat VIX historically favors short-covering bounces at support, and level_thesis's own primary scenario is bullish (support hold → 696.53). The single most important thing to watch at open: whether 691.66 is tested AND holds before 09:55 ET — a hold + bounce above 693.00 within the first 25 minutes directly invalidates the bearish consensus and triggers the bullish scenario.

technical.one_line: CLEAN_BEAR ribbon (72c spread) with two extreme-volume red closes (121k/204k); price 112c below pivot; bar structure mixed but final 2 bars decisively bearish

macro.one_line: VIX 17.86 MID/flat with zero scheduled events — clean technical tape; yesterday's weak close (SPY -0.41%) + BEAR ribbon persist into open with no overnight relief

level_thesis.one_line: Battle level 691.66 (prior RTH low, 2★ Active support) 45c below; asymmetry favors break — only 1★ resistance above until Carry 696.53 at +442 bps

internals.one_line: XLK -0.88% leading downside vs XLF -0.28% — narrow tech weakness is a SPY headwind; rotation signal mixed, not panic

validator.top_invalidation: Support hold at 691.66 + bounce above 693.00 by 09:55 ET flips entire consensus to bullish per level_thesis's own primary scenario
